mentions.note: Cyber security leader fell ~$100 from November highs; Jensen Wong praised it at CES as secure foundation of AI transformation; pounce when quality drops this far.
Cybersecurity consolidator, down 30%; top two in space; so important customers won't risk AI disruption; bought into dip; decline way out of whack with prospects; own for Travel Trust.

Crushed on Anthropic cybersecurity release, but Claude won't replace endpoint detection—different mission; CEO Kurtz explains Claude builds code security, CrowdStrike does threat response; PE under pressure but business unassailable.

Expensive but stand by it for Travel Trust; talented outfit; doesn't compete with Anthropic despite price compression; expertise will matter again, stock will go higher but maybe not in time for earnings.

Mission-critical cyber security with proprietary threat intelligence moat. AI agents cannot replicate first-time-final accuracy required for breach prevention. Stock rallied 4% today but remains down 13% YTD despite excellent quarter.
AI agentic security is largest long-term demand driver, not disruption risk. AgentWorks platform, proprietary data moat, customer urgency all present. Owned in Chap Trust; wall street wrong on fear.

Best-in-class cybersecurity despite Anthropic's baseless claims about eventual capability. Truth that Anthropic isn't hands-on security-qualified hasn't mattered to stock; misinformation dominates near-term. Hold conviction despite interim pain.
CEO Kurtz affirms AI agents increase cybersecurity demand, not threat. Stock sold off unfairly on Anthropic hype; AI vulnerabilities require traditional security.
Cybersecurity leader; George Kurtz directly refuted AI displacement risk; Cramer owns in Charitable Trust; better cybersecurity exposure than Zscaler.
Weeks of rumor Anthropic would destroy CRWD; today Anthropic announced partnership (Project Glasswing) with CRWD, Palo Alto Networks; Anthropic needs CRWD; stock +24 points, 'a lot more ahead.'

Anthropic partnership on Mythos AI (finding zero-day vulnerabilities) validates CrowdStrike's strategic position. Not disrupted by AI; required partner. Net-data creator (15+ year moat) enables AI training & defense without guardrails. AI adoption depends on security.
Cybersecurity (not traditional software) but caught in software ETF rotation; down 7% due to index mechanics.
Cybersecurity turbocharged by AI; new vulnerabilities create demand; up 6% today; misclassified in software ETF; well off highs.
AI-driven cybersecurity budgets expanding, not contracting; Anthropic Methos actually a tailwind; KeyBank upgrade to overweight validates thesis; stock rallied 4% despite down market.
Double-bottom reversal pattern; strong institutional money flow; currently $530s, likely $560s near-term, then $600 by year-end; Cramer loves.
Going power ball with strong momentum; bullwark against cyber criminals especially after Anthropic release scare; likely to handle profit-taking on strong earnings.

Record new ARR and accelerating revenue growth; raised full-year guidance by 520 basis points. AIDR technology up 250% QoQ. Leading ecosystem play on Glass Wing and Quilt Works—buying opportunity despite market weakness.
Sold off after Q1 but mythos scare will drive Q2+ adoption as long-cycle sales materialize; second half outlook strong.
